events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Voltage-rated rubber insulating gloves are of the wrong class for the system voltage present.",
    "Gloves are past their required electrical retest date per OSHA 1910.137 and NFPA 70E."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove defective/overdue gloves from service and tag out of use.",
    "Verify system nominal voltage and select gloves with appropriate class and maximum use voltage per ASTM D120 and NFPA 70E Table 130.7(C)(7)(a).",
    "Ensure gloves have current test date within 6 months per OSHA 1910.137(b)(2)(viii) before reissue.",
    "Perform air test and visual inspection on replacement gloves before each use."
  ],
  "rationale": "Using the wrong class of voltage-rated gloves or gloves with an expired test date exposes the worker to shock and arc-flash hazards. This is a non-compliant, installed-defective condition that must be corrected before energized work proceeds. Per NFPA 70E and OSHA 1910.137, gloves must be electrically tested at intervals not exceeding 6 months and must be rated for the system voltage.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 70E Article 130.7(C)(7)",
    "OSHA 1910.137(b)(2)(viii)",
    "OSHA 1910.335(a)(1)(i)",
    "ASTM D120"
  ]
}
